result:
All fixes are implemented and verified against a real production build (`npx next build && next start`). Here's what was wrong and what I changed:

**Root cause:** Lumina used to be "InboxZap" (email cleanup tool) and pivoted to an AI meeting assistant. The rebuild after the pivot was incomplete and broke most of the site's discovery paths.

**Fixed:**
1. **Title/meta/OG/Twitter tags** (`app/layout.tsx`) — were still branded "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while the actual page content is 100% about Lumina. This mismatch is almost certainly why GSC shows zero query volume for anything meeting-related despite that being the whole site. Now consistent, plus added `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
2. **12 dead blog URLs** — `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` were 404ing despite still being indexed with up to 3,900 impressions each (no route existed for them post-rebuild). Added 301 redirects to `/blog` instead of leaving them as hard 404s.
3. **Broken blog system** — the index page fetched a nonexistent `/api/posts` (showed "Could not load posts" to every visitor and every crawler), and there were zero replacement posts for the new business. Rebuilt it as a server-rendered index + `/blog/[slug]` route with 4 new posts targeting real content gaps (meeting minutes, AI vs. manual notes, action items, async standups) — topics GSC shows the site currently has *no* coverage for.
4. **`/features` and `/pricing`** — linked from the primary nav and the homepage's only CTA button, but both 404'd. Built out real pages.
5. **`/faq`** redirected to `/#faq`, an anchor that didn't exist. Added an actual FAQ section with `id="faq"` plus `FAQPage` JSON-LD.
6. **Sitemap** — was hardcoded to include the two dead pages and explicitly excluded blog posts (`// TODO`). Now dynamically includes all real pages.
7. Removed the OG image reference that pointed at a nonexistent file.

I verified all of this by running the actual Next.js build/server locally (the live URL you gave me hangs on every request — matches a known harness quirk, not something in this codebase) and curling every route, redirect, and metadata tag.

**Not done, worth a decision from you:** the 4 new blog posts are genuinely written content I authored to fill the keyword gap, not recovered originals — there's no CMS or content source in this repo for the old 12 posts, so I couldn't restore their actual bodies. Worth deciding whether to invest in more content on this same topic cluster, since organic traffic here is currently earned almost entirely off residual "inboxzap" brand-name searches rather than the product Lumina actually is today.
